result.name: SSL/TLS: Certificate Expired
result.description: The SSL/TLS certificate on this host has expired on 2025-12-01. Expired certificates are no longer trusted and may allow man-in-the-middle attacks.

nvt.name: SSL/TLS: Certificate Expired
nvt.solution: Renew the SSL/TLS certificate from your certificate authority and deploy it on the affected host.

result.name: Apache HTTP Server: Multiple Vulnerabilities (Apr 2025)
result.description: Apache HTTP Server versions prior to 2.4.59 are affected by multiple vulnerabilities including remote code execution via mod_rewrite and server-side request forgery.

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server: Multiple Vulnerabilities (Apr 2025)
nvt.solution: Update Apache HTTP Server to version 2.4.59 or later.

result.name: OpenSSH: User Enumeration Vulnerability
result.description: OpenSSH before 9.6 allows remote attackers to enumerate valid usernames by observing timing differences in authentication responses.

nvt.name: OpenSSH: User Enumeration Vulnerability
nvt.solution: Upgrade OpenSSH to version 9.6 or later.

result.name: PostgreSQL: Privilege Escalation via Row Security Policies
result.description: PostgreSQL versions before 16.2, 15.6, 14.11, 13.14, and 12.18 contain a vulnerability where authenticated users can escalate privileges through crafted row security policies.

nvt.name: PostgreSQL: Privilege Escalation via Row Security Policies
nvt.solution: Update PostgreSQL to version 16.2, 15.6, 14.11, 13.14, or 12.18 respectively.

result.name: TLS: Weak Cipher Suites Supported
result.description: The remote host supports weak SSL/TLS cipher suites including RC4 and DES-based ciphers which are considered cryptographically weak.

nvt.name: TLS: Weak Cipher Suites Supported
nvt.solution: Disable weak cipher suites in the TLS configuration. Only allow AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305, and other modern ciphers.

result.name: SMB: Signing Not Required
result.description: The remote SMB server does not require message signing. An attacker could exploit this to perform man-in-the-middle attacks against SMB connections.

nvt.name: SMB: Signing Not Required
nvt.solution: Enable mandatory SMB signing via Group Policy: Computer Configuration > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > Microsoft network server: Digitally sign communications (always) = Enabled.

result.name: DNS Server: Zone Transfer Allowed (AXFR)
result.description: The remote DNS server allows zone transfers to any host. This exposes internal network topology, hostnames, and IP addresses to potential attackers.

nvt.name: DNS Server: Zone Transfer Allowed (AXFR)
nvt.solution: Restrict zone transfers to authorized secondary DNS servers only using allow-transfer ACLs.

result.name: Nginx: Information Disclosure via Server Header
result.description: The remote Nginx web server exposes its version number in the Server HTTP response header (nginx/1.18.0). This information can help attackers identify known vulnerabilities for this specific version.

nvt.name: Nginx: Information Disclosure via Server Header
nvt.solution: Add 'server_tokens off;' to the Nginx configuration to suppress the version number in response headers.

result.name: MariaDB: Authentication Bypass (CVE-2025-21490)
result.description: MariaDB Server versions before 11.4.5 and 10.11.12 are vulnerable to an authentication bypass that allows remote unauthenticated attackers to gain access to the database server.

nvt.name: MariaDB: Authentication Bypass (CVE-2025-21490)
nvt.solution: Update MariaDB to version 11.4.5 or 10.11.12 or later.

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
result.description: The remote host responds to ICMP timestamp requests. This allows an attacker to determine the date and time set on the target machine, which may assist in further attacks.

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.solution: Filter incoming ICMP timestamp requests (type 13) at the firewall level.
